what_it_measures: Oxygen-carrying protein in red blood cells
low_means: Anemia — possible iron deficiency, B12/folate deficiency, blood loss, or chronic disease
high_means: Polycythemia — dehydration, lung disease, or rarely a blood disorder

what_it_measures: Total count of immune cells fighting infection
low_means: Leukopenia — viral infection, bone marrow issue, or medication side effect
high_means: Leukocytosis — bacterial infection, inflammation, stress, or leukemia

what_it_measures: Cell fragments that help blood clot
low_means: Thrombocytopenia — dengue fever, ITP, or bone marrow suppression
high_means: Thrombocytosis — infection, inflammation, or iron deficiency

what_it_measures: Blood sugar level after 8–10 hours of fasting
low_means: Hypoglycemia — skip meals, medication overdose
high_means: Prediabetes (100–125) or Diabetes (≥126) — requires follow-up

what_it_measures: Average blood sugar over the last 2–3 months
low_means: Not clinically significant unless very low (hypoglycemia risk)
high_means: 5.7–6.4% = Prediabetes; ≥6.5% = Diabetes; >8% = Poor control
preparation: No fasting needed. Can be done any time.

what_it_measures: Pituitary hormone that signals the thyroid to produce hormones
low_means: Hyperthyroidism — overactive thyroid (weight loss, fast heart rate, anxiety)
high_means: Hypothyroidism — underactive thyroid (fatigue, weight gain, cold intolerance)
preparation: No special preparation. Best collected in morning.

what_it_measures: Active thyroid hormone — regulates metabolism
low_means: Hypothyroidism, malnutrition, severe illness
high_means: Hyperthyroidism, T3 toxicosis

what_it_measures: Main thyroid hormone — converted to T3 in tissues
low_means: Primary or secondary hypothyroidism
high_means: Hyperthyroidism or excessive thyroid medication

what_it_measures: Total amount of cholesterol in blood
low_means: Rarely an issue; can indicate malnutrition
high_means: 200–239 = Borderline high; ≥240 = High — risk of heart disease
preparation: Fast for 9–12 hours before the test

what_it_measures: Low-density lipoprotein — deposits cholesterol in arteries
low_means: Good — lower is better
high_means: 100–129 = Near optimal; 130–159 = Borderline; ≥160 = High risk for heart disease

what_it_measures: High-density lipoprotein — removes cholesterol from arteries
low_means: Increased heart disease risk — below 40 (male) or 50 (female) is concerning
high_means: Higher is better — above 60 is protective

what_it_measures: Blood fat from food and body fat stores
low_means: Not clinically significant
high_means: 150–199 = Borderline; ≥200 = High — risk of pancreatitis and heart disease
preparation: Fast for 9–12 hours; avoid alcohol for 24 hours

what_it_measures: Storage form of Vitamin D — bone health and immunity
low_means: < 20 = Deficiency — bone pain, fatigue, depression, immunity issues
high_means: > 100 = Toxicity — rare but causes nausea, kidney issues

what_it_measures: Essential vitamin for nerve function and red blood cell production
low_means: < 200 = Deficiency — numbness, tingling, fatigue, anemia, memory issues
high_means: > 900 = Can indicate liver disease or certain cancers (rare)

what_it_measures: Iron storage protein — best indicator of iron stores
low_means: Iron deficiency — fatigue, hair loss, shortness of breath, poor concentration
high_means: Hemochromatosis, liver disease, chronic inflammation

what_it_measures: Liver enzyme — released when liver cells are damaged
low_means: Not clinically significant
high_means: Liver inflammation — fatty liver, hepatitis, alcohol damage, medication toxicity

what_it_measures: Enzyme found in liver, heart, and muscles
low_means: Not significant
high_means: Liver or heart damage, muscle injury, or intense exercise

what_it_measures: Waste product filtered by kidneys — indicates kidney health
low_means: Muscle wasting or low muscle mass
high_means: Kidney disease or dehydration — needs urgent evaluation
preparation: Avoid excessive meat intake 24 hours before

what_it_measures: Estimated filtration rate of kidneys — stages kidney disease
low_means: < 60 = Kidney disease; < 15 = Kidney failure
high_means: Higher is better
preparation: Calculated from creatinine — no additional test needed

what_it_measures: Breakdown product of purines — linked to gout and kidney stones
low_means: Rarely significant
high_means: Gout, kidney stones, or metabolic syndrome — avoid red meat, alcohol
preparation: Fast for 4 hours. Avoid alcohol 24 hours before.

what_it_measures: Marker of inflammation or infection
low_means: Normal — no significant inflammation
high_means: Active infection, autoimmune disease, or cardiovascular risk

what_it_measures: Rate at which RBCs settle — non-specific inflammation marker
low_means: Not significant
high_means: Infection, autoimmune disease, anemia, or cancer
